Title: The ethylene-responsive transcription factor PpERF9 represses PpRAP2.4 and PpMYB114 via histone deacetylation to inhibit anthocyanin biosynthesis in pear

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: fruit coloration; plant defense against biotic and abiotic stresses; visual signal for seed dispersers
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AP2/ERF family, Class II ERF, EAR motif-containing repressor with two EAR motifs
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Ethylene-inducible; peaks 1 d after ethephon treatment and slightly decreases after 1-MCP treatment in pear fruit
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by ethephon; repressed slightly by 1-MCP; analyzed in fruit peel and calli under light/UV-B conditions
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly represses PpMYB114 promoter activity and represses PpRAP2.4 promoter activity
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional repression; histone deacetylation via co-repressor recruitment
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms a repressor complex with PpTPL1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Ethylene-induced repressor that directly and indirectly suppresses the key anthocyanin activator PpMYB114, making it a major switch for ethylene-mediated anthocyanin inhibition
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Acts within a PpERF9-PpRAP2.4-PpMYB114 circuit; indirectly participates in negative feedback because PpRAP2.4 promotes ethylene biosynthesis that induces PpERF9
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: PpERF9 recruits PpTPL1 via EAR motifs to decrease histone H3 acetylation (H3ac) at PpRAP2.4 and PpMYB114 loci; TSA partly rescues anthocyanin accumulation; mutating both EAR motifs abolishes PpTPL1 interaction and histone deacetylation effect
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Inhibits anthocyanin accumulation and red coloration in pear fruit and calli
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpERF9 is induced by ethylene and encodes a nuclear transcriptional repressor. 2. It directly binds the GCC-box of the PpMYB114 promoter and represses PpMYB114 expression. 3. It also binds the DRE motif of the PpRAP2.4 promoter and represses PpRAP2.4, forming a PpERF9-PpRAP2.4-PpMYB114 circuit. 4. Through EAR motif-dependent interaction with PpTPL1, PpERF9 promotes histone H3 deacetylation at target loci and suppresses anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in fruit; VIGS; stable overexpression in pear calli; Y1H; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; dual-luciferase assay; transcriptional activity assay; subcellular localization; yeast two-hybrid; BiFC; TSA inhibitor treatment; EAR motif mutagenesis
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression constructs; native PpMYB114 and PpRAP2.4 promoters in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ethephon; 1-MCP; strong white light with UV-B; TSA treatment
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A promising target for reducing red pigmentation in pear; knockout/editing or repression of PpERF9 could enhance anthocyanin accumulation, especially under ethylene-rich postharvest or developmental contexts; deployment should consider interaction with ethylene signaling and chromatin regulation
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression decreases anthocyanin accumulation; possible broader ethylene-response and chromatin-regulation pleiotropy not fully resolved
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PpERF9 does not regulate PpMYB10 promoter activity in dual-luciferase assays; both EAR motifs are collectively required for PpTPL1 interaction and repression-associated histone deacetylation

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: fruit coloration; plant defense against biotic and abiotic stresses; visual signal for seed dispersers
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated by 1-MCP and not induced by ethephon in pear fruit
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Ethylene-repressed; higher under 1-MCP treatment; tested in fruit peel and calli under light/UV-B
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Suppressed by ethylene signaling; enhanced when ethylene perception is blocked by 1-MCP
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates PpMYB114 promoter and activates PpACS1 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive activator that rapidly promotes anthocyanin biosynthesis through activation of PpMYB114, especially when ethylene production/signaling is suppressed
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Forms a negative feedback loop: PpRAP2.4 activates PpACS1 and ethylene production, while ethylene/PpERF9 repress PpRAP2.4
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Its promoter/exon H3ac is reduced in PpERF9-OX and PpTPL1-OX backgrounds, indicating repression via histone deacetylation by upstream PpERF9-PpTPL1 complex
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation and red coloration in pear fruit; effect is stronger when ethylene action is blocked
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpRAP2.4 is an ethylene-repressed nuclear transcriptional activator. 2. It directly binds the GCC-box in the PpMYB114 promoter and activates PpMYB114 expression, promoting anthocyanin biosynthesis. 3. It also activates PpACS1, increasing ethylene production. 4. Its anthocyanin-promoting effect is partly counteracted by the ethylene/PpERF9 pathway, constituting a feedback circuit.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in fruit; VIGS; overexpression and RNAi in pear calli; Y1H; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay; subcellular localization; transcriptional activity assay; ethylene release measurements; 1-MCP treatment
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression constructs; native PpMYB114 and PpACS1 promoters in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 1-MCP; ethephon; strong white light with UV-B
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful positive target to enhance red coloration and anthocyanin accumulation in pear, particularly in low-ethylene contexts or combined with suppression of PpERF9; engineering should account for its stimulation of ethylene biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression increases ethylene release and callus growth rate, which may offset anthocyanin gains and affect broader ripening/growth traits
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PpRAP2.4 does not activate the PpMYB10 promoter in dual-luciferase assays, although PpMYB10 transcript levels changed in some overexpression/silencing contexts, likely indirectly

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Clapp's Favorite calli; Hongzaosu source cloning
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: fruit coloration; plant defense against biotic and abiotic stresses; visual signal for seed dispersers
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: pear calli; fruit peel context inferred
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TOPLESS co-repressor with LisH, CTLH, and WD40 repeat domains
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Function tested by overexpression and RNAi in pear calli
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Recruited by PpERF9 through EAR motifs
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses expression of PpRAP2.4 and PpMYB114 through histone deacetylation in complex with PpERF9
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms PpERF9-PpTPL1 repression complex; CTLH domain mediates interaction with PpERF9
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential co-repressor enabling PpERF9-mediated chromatin repression of key anthocyanin regulators
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Overexpression reduces H3ac at PpRAP2.4 and PpMYB114 loci, supporting a direct role in histone deacetylation-mediated repression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates anthocyanin accumulation and red coloration in pear calli
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpTPL1 is a nuclear TOPLESS co-repressor that physically interacts with PpERF9. 2. Its CTLH domain is sufficient for interaction with PpERF9, while PpERF9 EAR motifs are required. 3. Overexpression represses, and RNAi silencing promotes, anthocyanin accumulation in pear calli. 4. It decreases H3ac at PpRAP2.4 and PpMYB114 loci, linking chromatin repression to anthocyanin inhibition.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast two-hybrid; BiFC; subcellular localization; overexpression and RNAi in pear calli; anthocyanin quantification; qPCR; ChIP-qPCR for H3ac
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for releasing chromatin repression to increase pear anthocyanin accumulation, though broad pleiotropic effects are likely because TPL proteins are central co-repressors
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely broad developmental and signaling pleiotropy due to central co-repressor function; study focused mainly on anthocyanin phenotype in calli
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PpERF9 interacted with PpTPL1 but not PpSAP18, indicating co-repressor specificity in this system

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: fruit coloration; plant defense against biotic and abiotic stresses; visual signal for seed dispersers
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced by 1-MCP and repressed by ethephon; a major ethylene-responsive positive regulator
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression reduced in PpERF9-OX and PpTPL1-OX backgrounds and induced by PpRAP2.4 overexpression or PpERF9/PpTPL1 silencing
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Ethylene signaling; PpERF9 repression; PpRAP2.4 activation; chromatin acetylation state
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Anthocyanin biosynthetic structural genes including PpUFGT; PpANS; PpDFR; PpF3H; PpCHI; PpCHS
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes anthocyanin biosynthetic gene expression as a key activator
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions as a core MYB regulator of the MBW anthocyanin activation complex; competes functionally with repressor network discussed in paper
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key positive regulator whose repression is central to ethylene-mediated inhibition of anthocyanin biosynthesis in pear
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: H3ac at promoter/proximal promoter/first exon decreases upon PpERF9 or PpTPL1 overexpression and after ethephon treatment; increases after 1-MCP treatment
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation; reduced expression leads to decreased red coloration
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpMYB114 is a major positive regulator of pear anthocyanin biosynthesis. 2. It is directly repressed by PpERF9 and activated by PpRAP2.4 via promoter binding to the same GCC-box region. 3. Ethylene reduces its expression partly through PpERF9-PpTPL1-mediated histone deacetylation. 4. Its downregulation is a central cause of reduced anthocyanin accumulation in pear.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression analysis under ethephon/1-MCP; PpERF9 and PpRAP2.4 Y1H/EMSA/dual-luciferase targeting assays; ChIP-qPCR for PpERF9 binding and H3ac status; correlation with overexpression/silencing phenotypes of upstream regulators
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native PpMYB114 promoter used in Y1H and dual-luciferase assays
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A valuable positive regulatory node for enhancing red pear coloration, potentially via promoter editing to weaken PpERF9 repression or via stacking with positive regulators while considering network balance
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although not the primary manipulated gene in this paper, it is a central experimentally validated target within the core regulatory circuit and was repeatedly assayed as the decisive anthocyanin activator
